summary: Preinstall hook launches GUI calculator on npm install — proof-of-concept dependency-confusion artifact
details: package.json declares `preinstall: bun run index.js`. On `npm install`, index.js detects the platform and execSyncs a GUI calculator: `open -a Calculator` on macOS, `xcalc || gnome-calculator || kcalc` on Linux, `calc.exe` on Windows (index.js lines 9-17). The package self-describes as an HTTP file uploader, so spawning a calculator at install time is unrelated to its advertised purpose and is the canonical `calc.exe` proof-of-concept used in dependency-confusion / supply-chain research demos. The hook also requires the Bun runtime to be pre-installed, further confirming it is an experimental artifact rather than functioning software. Metadata is low-effort (author `sleep`, homepage `git.hfaf.com/urlaa`). While the current payload is benign (a calculator window), the structure — preinstall → arbitrary execSync of platform-specific commands — is exactly the surface a malicious update could swap for a real payload, and it executes attacker-defined commands on installer machines without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
